evals.name: synthesize-after-work
evals.prompt: I just wrapped up the retry work on this branch (feat/retry-backoff) and I'm about to open a PR. Package up a handoff doc for whoever picks up the PR review. What happened: I added exponential backoff to fetch() capped at 3 attempts. I deviated from the original plan of a fixed 1s delay — went with doubling backoff (0.5s -> 1s -> 2s) because the upstream API rate-limits in bursts. Important: the retry only catches TransientError on purpose — don't broaden it to a bare except, that would mask real bugs. Also the tests in tests/test_fetch.py are still placeholders, and CI lint will likely flag the unused `time` import. The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: Writes the branch-keyed handoff at .claude/handoffs/feat/retry-backoff.md under the repo root — the branch name's slash is preserved as a real subdirectory (feat/ is a directory), NOT flattened into a single filename. Creates the .claude/handoffs/feat/ parent directory as needed. Fills YAML frontmatter and all 8 sections from the narrative (backoff decision, deviation from fixed delay, the TransientError gotcha, placeholder tests / lint as verification + open threads). Adds the directory entry .claude/handoffs/ to .git/info/exclude. Does not commit the file. Does not paste raw diffs.

evals.name: read-existing-handoff
evals.prompt: I'm picking up this PR branch cold. Read the handoff the previous agent left and catch me up — what shipped, what I should be careful not to undo, and what's still open. The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: Reads the branch-keyed handoff at .claude/handoffs/<branch>.md (the path derived from the current branch, slashes kept as subdirectories) and summarizes it: cursor pagination shipped, the deliberate gotcha (do not embed page size in the cursor), and open threads (no backward pagination). Does not modify the handoff file and does not create new files.

evals.name: read-missing-handoff
evals.prompt: Read the handoff doc for this branch and summarize what the last agent did. The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: Checks the branch-keyed path .claude/handoffs/<branch>.md, finds nothing there (and no matching legacy file), and reports that no handoff doc is present and that the caller should proceed without it. Does not fabricate a summary and does not create a handoff file.

evals.name: read-legacy-fallback-branch-aware
evals.prompt: Read the handoff for this branch and catch me up on what the last agent did. There's no .claude/handoffs/ doc yet, but there's an older .claude/agent-handoff.md left over from before. The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: The branch-keyed .claude/handoffs/<branch>.md is absent, so it falls back to the legacy .claude/agent-handoff.md — but only after checking that file's `branch:` frontmatter. It uses the legacy doc only when that stamp equals the current branch, then summarizes what shipped, the gotchas, and open threads. It must state that a MISMATCHED branch stamp is treated as no handoff present (the stale legacy doc from another branch is never served, to avoid a wrong-branch context leak). Does not migrate, rewrite, or modify the legacy file, and does not create new files.
